title: Demo apps for 500-node conference mesh
description:
## Goal

Five demo applications showcasing what a 500-node mesh can do that nothing else can.

## The apps

1. **Find My Hacker** - Live GPS map of all nodes
2. **DEF CON Confessions** - Anonymous mesh message board
3. **Mesh Trivia** - Real-time crowd voting/games
4. **Dead Drop** - Encrypted store-and-forward messages
5. **Chaos Telemetry** - Live mesh vital signs dashboard

## Architecture

All apps share:
- Gateway aggregates data from mesh
- Web frontend on big screen + mobile
- CoAP/MQTT-SN from nodes to gateway
- Node-side: minimal, runs on T-Echo

```
[500 T-Echos] --LoRa--> [4 Gateways] --WiFi--> [Demo Server] --HDMI--> [Big Screen]
                                          |
                                     [Mobile Web]
```

## Shared infrastructure

- Gateway CoAP server (already exists)
- WebSocket bridge: CoAP → browser
- Web dashboard framework (D3.js or similar)
- Node firmware: report position, receive commands, display messages

## Privacy by design

- No persistent logs (RAM only, or short TTL)
- No accounts or identity tracking
- Opt-out for position sharing
- E2E encryption for dead drops
- Confessions are anonymous (no source tracking)
notes:
## P2P vs Gateway Summary

### Runs forever without gateway (core firmware features)
| Feature | P2P capability |
|---------|----------------|
| Position sharing | Coords in announces, local e-ink map |
| Dead Drop | Epidemic store-and-forward, DTN |
| Direct chat | Node-to-node encrypted messaging |
| Local stats | Self-telemetry on e-ink |
| Crush (proximity) | BLE exchange + local matching |
| Broadcast chat | Gossip with TTL |

### Gateway-enhanced (adds scale + visualization)
| Feature | Gateway adds |
|---------|--------------|
| Find My Hacker | Aggregated map of 500 nodes |
| Confessions | Big screen feed, moderation |
| Trivia | Mesh-wide polls, results dashboard |
| Dead Drop | Reliable storage, higher delivery rate |
| Telemetry | Aggregated dashboard, alerts |
| Crush | Mesh-wide matching, not just proximity |

### Design principle
**Gateway enhances but is never required.** Two nodes in the woods can:
- Message each other
- Share positions
- Dead drop with TTL
- Crush on proximity
- See local mesh stats

Gateway adds scale, persistence, and visualization for events/deployments.

### Firmware implications
These P2P features should be in base firmware:
- Position in announces (already spec'd)
- DTN store-and-forward (already spec'd)
- Direct messaging (already spec'd)
- Broadcast chat (add message type)
- Local telemetry display (UI feature)
- BLE crush exchange (new feature)

Gateway apps are separate services that consume/enhance these primitives.
